channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: UK Reviews Pakistani Asylum Cases Amid Concerns Over Low Deportation Rate
item.description: Recently, Pakistan has come under attention from the United Kingdom as concerns grow over the rising number of asylum applications from Pakistani nationals who initially enter Britain through legal vi...

item.title: Domestic LPG Price Increased and Nepal’s RSP Surges in Elections Amid Global Tensions
item.description: On March 7, 2026, domestic LPG prices in India saw a sharp increase of ₹60 per cylinder, with the new price for a non-subsidised 14.2-kg cylinder now at ₹913 in Delhi, as energy prices rise due to ong...

item.title: Metropolitan Police interview three women under caution in Mohamed al Fayed abuse facilitation probe
item.description: The Metropolitan Police have interviewed three women under caution as part of an ongoing investigation into allegations linked to the late Mohamed al Fayed, the former Harrods owner who died in 2023. ...

item.title: PRC Announces Results of February 2026 Nurses Licensure Exam
item.description: The Professional Regulation Commission (PRC) has officially released the results of the February 2026 Nurses Licensure Examination conducted by the Board of Nursing across 21 testing centers in the Ph...

item.title: Kenya Airways Evacuates Stranded Passengers From Middle East, Lands Safely in Nairobi
item.description: A Kenya Airways special repatriation flight safely touched down at Jomo Kenyatta International Airport, bringing back dozens of passengers stranded in the Middle East amid escalating tensions. The ope...

item.title: M-Pesa Reaches 40 Million Users as Mobile Finance Expands in Kenya
item.description: Safaricom's mobile money platform, M-Pesa, has seen significant growth, adding 6 million new users in 2026 to hit a total of 40 million monthly active users in Kenya. Since its launch on March 6, 2007...

item.title: The Evolution of Frankenstein's Bride as a Feminist Figure in Cinema
item.description: Frankenstein’s Bride, the female creature first seen on screen in James Whale’s 1935 film The Bride of Frankenstein, has become a lasting symbol of rebellion and feminist autonomy. Played memorably by...

item.title: Fake Cleaning Products Sold to Pensioners and Bargain Seekers in Gauteng
item.description: Authorities in Gauteng have uncovered operations where counterfeit cleaning products are being manufactured and sold to unsuspecting consumers, particularly targeting pensioners and bargain hunters. T...

item.title: TotalEnergies Hands Over OLO Oilfield Community Trust Responsibilities to Aradel Holdings
item.description: As per the Petroleum Industry Act (PIA), TotalEnergies don officially transfer the OLO Oilfield Host Community Development Trust (HCDT) to Aradel Holdings Plc. This one na follow Aradel acquisition of...

item.title: UN calls for urgent investigation after deadly strike on Iranian girls’ school
item.description: The United Nations has strongly urged a swift and transparent investigation into a deadly strike on the Shajare Tayyebeh elementary school in Minab, southern Iran, which killed at least 150 people. Th...

item.title: Iranian Drone Intercepted Over Bahrain Amid US-Israel-Iran Tensions
item.description: On March 7, 2026, tensions escalated in the Middle East as an Iranian drone was intercepted over the Bahrain Financial Harbour towers, where the Israeli embassy is located. This incident comes amid on...

item.title: Aston Martin faces design and reliability issues ahead of new F1 season
item.description: Aston Martin’s Formula One team is encountering significant challenges ahead of the new F1 season, despite high expectations. Designer Adrian Newey, hired at great cost by Canadian owner Lawrence Stro...

item.title: Tinubu Government Plans Ambassadorial Postings Sparking Debate Over Career Diplomats Assigned Mostly to Africa While Political Appointees Get Europe, Asia and Americas
item.description: The Bola Tinubu administration dey set to submit fresh list of ambassadorial nominees to the Ministry of Foreign Affairs next week, but the matter don already cause big wahala inside diplomatic circle...

item.title: Evaluating Noam Chomsky’s Intellectual Legacy Amid Epstein Revelations
item.description: The recent revelation of Noam Chomsky’s association with the convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein has surprised many who admired Chomsky for his strong critique of US neo-imperialism and domestic el...

item.title: Nine Villagers from Adanla in Kwara Released After N40 Million Ransom Payment
item.description: Nine people from Adanla community for Ifelodun Local Government Area, Kwara State don regain their freedom after dem don pay over N40 million ransom. Dem get kidnapped on December 26, 2025, when armed...

item.title: Environmental and Resource Challenges in Venezuela's Orinoco River Basin
item.description: Venezuela’s Orinoco River Basin is one of the most biodiverse regions in the world, home to river dolphins, endangered Orinoco crocodiles, and over a thousand species of freshwater fish and birds. How...

item.title: ATOM clarifies it will not join the UniTeam coalition
item.description: In a recent clarification, the August Twenty-One Movement (ATOM) confirmed that it will not form a coalition with the so-called 'UniTeam' or a 'uni-yellow' alliance. This statement comes amidst ongoin...

item.title: RCMP investigates disappearance of Iranian activist in Burnaby
item.description: The B.C. RCMP has initiated a homicide investigation into the disappearance of Masood Masjoody, an Iranian activist and former math instructor at Simon Fraser University, who went missing in early Feb...

item.title: Australian government bans Hizb ut-Tahrir under new hate speech legislation
item.description: The Australian government has officially banned the Islamist organisation Hizb ut-Tahrir, making it a criminal offence to be a member or provide financial support to the group. This move comes under n...

item.title: Kenya Urged to Intensify Preparations for 2026 Women's Africa Cup of Nations
item.description: The editorial calls on Kenyan football authorities to thoroughly prepare the Harambee Starlets for the upcoming Women's Africa Cup of Nations (WAFCON) in 2026. It stresses that after qualifying, the t...
